points.speaker: Patricia Henslow
points.text: Staff issued 18 email updates reaching 913 subscribers per email and will start outreach via Mountain View Voice for the Housing and Eviction Help Center. Mediation remains strong with 47 mediations/conciliations and 36 resolved. Staff observed an uptick in failure-to-pay notices and will analyze new versus repeat cases next month.
points.source: Patricia Henslow : We've had 18 e-mail updates for reach of 913 e-mail subscribers per e-mail... will begin starting our Mountain View Voice outreach... We've had 47 mediations and conciliations so far 36 of those have been resolved... We've seen a slight uptick in failure to pay rent notices... we will next month.

points.speaker: Patricia Henslow
points.text: Year-to-date notices: 136 rent increase notices, 278 termination notices, 0 tenant buyouts, and 0 additional occupant notices. No new properties have entered redevelopment this calendar year; two households received assistance as carryovers from prior year actions. Nine tenant petitions were filed (4 unlawful rent, 5 tenant hardship).
points.source: Patricia Henslow : We've received 136 banks rent increases notices, 278 termination notices, 0 tenant buyouts and zero additional occupant notices... two households have received assistance and that those two households are carryovers from prior calendar year redevelopment actions... We've had nine tenant petitions... four for unlawful rent and five for tenant hardship.

points.speaker: Almond
points.text: Almond thanked the City for volunteer appreciation benefits and described attending a Shoreline concert with VIP amenities, noting an intention to send a thank-you letter. The meeting adjourned at 8:57 PM, with the next RHC meeting scheduled for Monday, November 14, 2022, at 7:00 PM.
points.source: Almond : I felt very appreciated and I'm going to send a letter to the city and thank them. Nicole Haynes : the meeting is adjourned at 8:57 PM... next Rental Housing committee meeting is scheduled to be held on Monday, November 14th, 2022 at 7:00 PM.

decisions.text: Approved consent calendar items 3.1 and 3.3 covering September meeting minutes and CSFRA regulation amendments by unanimous vote.
Authorized continued virtual meetings pursuant to Government Code Section 54953 by four votes to one.
decisions.source: Unidentified 1 : Motion passes four votes to 1

decisions.text: Adopted amendments to CSFRA Chapter 5 and MHRSO Chapter 6 hearing procedures to add compliance hearing processes and 30-day notice prior to rent restoration. Vote passed 4 ayes, 1 abstention.
decisions.source: Nicole Haynes : a motion to approve should include... adopt the amendment to the CSFRA Regulation Chapter 5... and... MHRSO Regulations Chapter 6... Unidentified 1 : The motion passes four votes and one abstention.

validation_details.reasoning: Transcript clearly confirms Patricia Henslow provided all the specific details mentioned: 18 email updates, 913 subscribers, Mountain View Voice outreach plans, 47 mediations with 36 resolved, and uptick in failure-to-pay notices with analysis planned for next month.
